title: IT project managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate activities in such fields as electronic data processing, information systems, systems analysis, and computer programming. Analyze and coordinate the schedule, timeline, procurement, staffing, and budget of a product or service on a per project basis. Lead and guide the work of technical staff. May serve as a point of contact for the client or customer. Design and implement computer and information networks, such as local area networks (LAN), wide area networks (WAN), intranets, extranets, and other data communications networks. Perform network modeling, analysis, and planning, including analysis of capacity needs for network infrastructures. May also design network and computer security measures. May research and recommend network and data communications hardware and software. (Based on 21 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Meet with department heads, managers, supervisors, vendors, and others to solicit cooperation and resolve problems; consult with users, management, vendors, and technicians to assess computing needs and system requirements; consult with customers or other departments on project status, proposals, or technical issues, such as software system design or maintenance; communicate with network personnel or Web site hosting agencies to address hardware or software issues affecting Web sites.

Purchase necessary equipment; order or maintain inventory of telecommunications equipment; prepare purchase requisitions for computer hardware and software, networking and telecommunications equipment, test equipment, cabling, or tools.

Prepare and review operational reports or project progress reports; prepare reports or correspondence concerning project specifications, activities, or status; document user support activity, such as system problems, corrective actions, resolution status, and completed equipment installations; document technical specifications and operating standards for telecommunications equipment; document procedures for hardware and software installation and use; prepare system activity and performance reports.

Evaluate data processing proposals to assess project feasibility and requirements; review project plans to plan and coordinate project activity; develop and interpret organizational goals, policies, and procedures; review and evaluate requests from engineers, managers, and technicians for system modifications; analyze information to determine, recommend, and plan installation of a new system or modification of an existing system; analyze user needs and software requirements to determine feasibility of design within time and cost constraints; confer with data processing or project managers to obtain information on limitations or capabilities for data processing projects.

Recruit, hire, train, and supervise staff, or participate in staffing decisions; assign and review the work of systems analysts, programmers, and other computer-related workers; supervise and assign work to programmers, designers, technologists, technicians, or other engineering or scientific personnel.

Control operational budget and expenditures; estimate costs for system or component implementation and operation; develop plans or budgets for network equipment replacement; monitor functioning of equipment to ensure system operates in conformance with specifications.

Evaluate the organization's technology use and needs and recommend improvements, such as hardware and software upgrades; stay abreast of advances in technology; communicate with telecommunications vendors to obtain pricing and technical specifications for available hardware, software, or services; research and test new or modified hardware or software products to determine performance and interoperability; develop or recommend network security measures, such as firewalls, network security audits, or automated security probes; monitor security system performance logs to identify problems and notify security specialists when problems occur.

Review and approve all systems charts and programs prior to their implementation; develop or direct software system testing or validation procedures, programming, or documentation; plan test schedules or strategies in accordance with project scope or delivery dates; conduct software compatibility tests with programs, hardware, operating systems, or network environments; document test plans, testing procedures, or test results; develop testing programs that address areas such as database impacts, software scenarios, regression testing, negative testing, error or bug retests, or usability; evaluate or recommend software for testing or bug tracking.

Develop computer information resources, providing for data security and control, strategic computing, and disaster recovery; develop or implement procedures for ongoing Web site revision; develop disaster recovery plans; coordinate network operations, maintenance, repairs, or upgrades; maintain networks by performing activities such as file addition, deletion, or backup; implement or perform preventive maintenance, backup, or recovery procedures; develop plans or budgets for network equipment replacement; store, retrieve, and manipulate data for analysis of system capabilities and requirements.

Modify existing software to correct errors, adapt it to new hardware, or upgrade interfaces and improve performance; design, develop and modify software systems, using scientific analysis and mathematical models to predict and measure outcomes and consequences of design; document software defects, using a bug tracking system, and report defects to software developers; implement system renovation projects in collaboration with technical staff, engineering consultants, installers, and vendors; design or develop automated testing tools.

Identify, analyze, and document problems with program function, output, online screen, or content; collaborate with field staff or customers to evaluate or diagnose problems and recommend possible solutions; respond to user email inquiries or set up automated systems to send responses; provide user support by diagnosing network and device problems and implementing technical or procedural solutions; train users to use new or modified equipment; communicate with system users to ensure accounts are set up properly or to diagnose and solve operational problems.

Prepare or monitor project schedules, budgets, or cost control systems; develop procedures to track, project, or report network availability, reliability, capacity, or utilization; participate in network technology upgrade or expansion projects, including installation of hardware and software and integration testing; prepare detailed network specifications, including diagrams, charts, equipment configurations, or recommended technologies.

Develop conceptual, logical, or physical network designs; design, build, or maintain network equipment configuration prototypes, including network hardware, software, servers, or server operating systems; design network-related documentation; develop network-related documentation; develop network security measures, such as firewalls, network security audits, or automated security probes.

Analyze and coordinate the schedule, timeline, procurement, staffing, and budget of a product or service on a per project basis; communicate with customers, sales staff, or marketing staff to determine customer needs; create Web models or prototypes that include physical, interface, logical, or data models; document technical factors such as server load, bandwidth, database performance, and browser and device types; monitor and analyze network performance and reports on data input or output to detect problems, identify inefficient use of computer resources, or perform capacity planning.

Train users to use new or modified equipment; supervise the work of programmers, technologists and technicians and other engineering and scientific personnel; monitor security system performance logs to identify problems and notify security specialists when problems occur.

Conduct historical analyses of test results; monitor bug resolution efforts and track successes; document test procedures to ensure replicability and compliance with standards; perform initial debugging procedures by reviewing configuration files, logs, or code pieces to determine breakdown source; visit beta testing sites to evaluate software performance; coordinate user or third-party testing; develop or maintain project reporting systems; develop databases that support Web applications and Web sites.

Supervise maintenance of telecommunications equipment; maintain or coordinate the maintenance of network peripherals, such as printers; implement controls to provide security for operating systems, software, and data; install and configure hypertext transfer protocol (HTTP) servers and associated operating systems; supervise and assign work to programmers, designers, technologists, technicians, or other engineering or scientific personnel; communicate with telecommunications vendors to obtain pricing and technical specifications for available hardware, software, or services; investigate customer problems referred by technical support; evaluate code to ensure that it is valid, is properly structured, meets industry standards, and is compatible with browsers, devices, or operating systems; back up files from Web sites to local directories for instant recovery in case of problems.
Design and implement computer and information networks, such as local area networks (LAN), wide area networks (WAN), intranets, extranets, and other data communications networks.

Provide feedback and recommendations to developers on software usability and functionality, identify problems uncovered by testing or customer feedback, and correct problems or refer them to appropriate personnel for resolution. Monitor program performance to ensure efficient and problem-free operations. Test programs or databases, correct errors, and make necessary modifications. Review project requests describing database user needs to estimate time and cost for project completion. Identify and evaluate industry trends in database systems to advise upper management. Establish and calculate optimum values for database parameters, using manuals and calculators.

Work as part of a project team to coordinate database development and determine project scope and limitations. Write and code logical and physical database descriptions, specifying identifiers for the management system or directing others in coding descriptions. Develop data models describing data elements and their use, following prescribed procedures and using software tools.

Develop or implement procedures, protocols, or policies for database management that support ongoing revision and maintenance of information security standards and best practices. Coordinate documentation of computer security or emergency measure policies and conduct investigations of information security breaches to identify vulnerabilities and evaluate damage. Develop response and recovery strategies for security breaches, as well as assess quality of security controls using performance indicators.

Meet with clients to discuss technical specifications, customized solutions, or operational problems. Provide technical support to computer users for installation and use of security products. Train staff on information security standards, policies, and best practices. Oversee performance of risk assessment or execution of system tests to ensure functioning of data processing activities or security measures. Coordinate monitoring of networks or systems for security breaches or intrusions.

Troubleshoot security and network problems, and identify or implement solutions to information security problems. Scan networks using vulnerability assessment tools to identify vulnerabilities and recommend information security enhancements to management. Document, design, code, or test various technologies that involve data processing or information systems. Evaluate new technologies or vendor products related to cybersecurity and provide clear, detailed descriptions of specifications related to them.

Maintain understanding of current web technologies or programming practices through continuing education, reading, or participation in professional conferences, workshops, or groups. Collaborate with management or users to develop strategies and integrate them with projects.

Conduct network modeling and analysis, including planning for network capacities. Communicate with network personnel or web hosting agencies to address hardware or software issues affecting systems. Conduct user research to determine design requirements and analyze user feedback to improve design quality. Design and implement network and computer security measures, and research and recommend hardware and software for network and data communications.

Identify or maintain links to and from other websites and check links to ensure proper functioning. Develop and document style guidelines for web content, creating searchable indices for web pages. Write and edit technical documentation for digital products, including user manuals and testing protocols.

Create visual representations of GIS data using complex procedures and develop methods for integrating various products for cohesive functionality. Perform geospatial data analysis to support scientific problems, and maintain or modify existing GIS databases or applications.

Perform forensic investigations on operational or file systems, and preserve and maintain digital forensic evidence for analysis. Conduct predictive or reactive analyses on security measures, and write technical summaries to report findings.
Evaluate new blockchain technologies and vendor products, as well as assess risks based on security assessments or control matrix reviews.

Prepare and submit reports describing the results of security fixes and maintain up-to-date knowledge of hacking trends. Write audit reports to communicate findings and maintain cyber defense software or hardware to support responses to cyber incidents.

Design and implement data repositories to integrate data, as well as develop and implement procedures for ongoing web site revision. Document penetration test findings and ensure adherence to technical standards in systems designs.
Gather cyber intelligence to identify vulnerabilities and assess the physical security of servers or systems. Develop policies for data collection and processing.
Identify, evaluate, and recommend hardware or software technologies to achieve desired performance, and provide technical support to junior staff or clients.
Set up database clusters, backup processes, and recovery plans; implement updates, upgrades, and patches in a timely manner.
Test new software packages for use in operations, and evaluate or recommend server hardware or software.
Develop and document testing routines and procedures, including conducting user testing to determine application effectiveness or usability.
Provide training and technical guidance for team members or clients regarding software and system usage, as well as oversee the installation of software and hardware components.
Develop or maintain standards for data management including database architectures, documentation of schemas, and verify structure and quality of data.
Create and enforce database development standards, and design database applications to ensure efficient access and system reliability.
Perform system analysis and collaborate with stakeholders to define system requirements and develop project plans that include budgets and schedules.
Direct the analysis, development, and operation of computer systems; monitor system performance to detect potential problems and ensure security measures are implemented.
Manage project communication by establishing a project plan, scheduling meetings, tracking milestones, and providing performance feedback to team members.
Conduct needs assessments to identify project requirements, consult with stakeholders, and negotiate with suppliers to obtain necessary resources.
Develop data processes, including extraction, transformation, and integrations for data warehouse systems, and provide test specifications for quality assurance.
Document technical functions and specifications for proposed systems, and create plans for improving content management system capabilities.
Monitor Web developments, implement security measures, and conduct regular testing to ensure systems are secure and efficient.
Administer internet and intranet infrastructure, including Web, file, and mail servers, and provide troubleshooting support.
Research or test new technologies and assist in the development of electronic document management systems and classification taxonomies.
Plan and install upgrades of database management system software, and perform ongoing maintenance operations, including hardware and software upgrades.
Collaborate with project personnel to identify and resolve issues during the project lifecycle, ensuring adherence to quality standards and deliverables.
Implement electronic document processing systems, and administer access rights and revision control to ensure system integrity.
Design, implement, or operate network systems, including LAN, WAN, and security measures to ensure reliable data communication.
Communicate project information through various channels, ensuring clarity and understanding among team members and stakeholders.
Gather and analyze user feedback or data to resolve issues or improve systems, and monitor compliance with regulatory requirements in document management.
Establish functional or system standards, and document installation or configuration procedures to maintain systems for future reference.
Provide guidelines for implementing secure systems and advise on project costs or design concepts.
Coordinate recruitment or selection of project personnel and assign duties, responsibilities, and spans of authority to project personnel.

Develop and manage work breakdown structure (WBS) of information technology projects and update project plans to include objectives, technologies, systems, specifications, schedules, funding, and staffing.
Manage project execution to ensure adherence to budget, schedule, and scope, and monitor project milestones, deliverables, and performance of project team members.

Prepare project status reports by collecting, analyzing, and summarizing information and trends and communicate project status including budget, resources, technical issues, or customer satisfaction to managers.
Plan, schedule, or coordinate project activities to meet deadlines, and schedule or facilitate project meetings.
Produce and distribute project documents and create project status presentations for delivery to customers or project personnel.
Research and identify project needs such as resources, staff, or finances by reviewing project objectives and schedules.
Negotiate with project stakeholders or suppliers to obtain resources or materials.
Request and review project updates to ensure deadlines are met and confer with project personnel to identify and resolve problems.
Solicit feedback from design and technical staff to integrate it into project plans.
